title: Drift-Based Policy Optimization: Native One-Step Policy Learning for Online Robot Control
abstract: Although multi-step generative policies achieve strong performance in robotic manipulation by modeling multimodal action distributions, they require multi-step iterative denoising at inference time. Each action therefore needs tens to hundreds of network function evaluations (NFEs), making them costly for high-frequency closed-loop control and online reinforcement learning (RL). To address this limitation, we propose a two-stage framework for native one-step generative policies that shifts refinement from inference to training. First, we introduce the Drift-Based Policy (DBP), which leverages fixed-point drifting objectives to internalize iterative refinement into the model parameters, yielding a one-step generative backbone by design while preserving multimodal action modeling capacity. Second, we develop Drift-Based Policy Optimization (DBPO), an online RL framework that equips the pretrained backbone with a compatible stochastic interface, enabling stable on-policy updates without sacrificing the one-step deployment property. Extensive experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed framework across offline imitation learning, online fine-tuning, and real-world control scenarios. DBP matches or exceeds the performance of multi-step diffusion policies while achieving up to $100\times$ faster inference. It also consistently outperforms existing one-step baselines on challenging manipulation benchmarks. Moreover, DBPO enables effective and stable policy improvement in online settings. Experiments on a real-world dual-arm robot demonstrate reliable high-frequency control at 105.2 Hz.
summary:
# 论文总结：Drift-Based Policy Optimization: Native One-Step Policy Learning for Online Robot Control

## 1. 核心创新点

论文提出以“将迭代细化从推理阶段转移到训练阶段”为核心思想的两阶段框架：先用 Drift-Based Policy (DBP) 构建原生 one-step 生成式策略骨干，再用 Drift-Based Policy Optimization (DBPO) 使其能够在保持一步部署特性的同时进行在线强化学习更新，从而兼顾多模态动作建模、推理效率与在线控制可用性。

## 2. 技术创新拆解

- **Drift-Based Policy：原生一步式生成策略骨干**：DBP 通过 fixed-point drifting objectives 将原本需要在推理时多步迭代完成的 refinement 内化进模型参数，目标是在保留 multimodal action distribution 建模能力的同时，将策略执行改写为 one-step 生成。
- **DBPO：面向在线机器人控制的策略优化框架**：DBPO 为预训练的 DBP 骨干设计了兼容的 stochastic interface，使其能够进行稳定的 on-policy 在线更新，并尽量不破坏 one-step deployment property，面向高频闭环控制和在线 RL 场景。

## 3. 方法论突破

- **新颖性**：区别于依赖多步 iterative denoising 的生成式策略，本文把“迭代求精”主要放到训练过程中完成，试图从架构层面原生获得 one-step policy learning，而不是事后压缩多步采样过程。
- **关键技术**：关键技术由两部分组成：一是用 fixed-point drifting objectives 训练 DBP 作为 one-step generative backbone；二是用与该骨干兼容的 stochastic interface 构建 DBPO，以支持在线 on-policy policy improvement。
- **理论支撑**：摘要提到了 fixed-point drifting objectives 与稳定的 on-policy updates，但未披露具体理论证明、定理表述、收敛性分析或稳定性条件。

## 4. 实验验证

- **主要基准/场景**：摘要称方法在 offline imitation learning、online fine-tuning、real-world control 以及 challenging manipulation benchmarks 上进行了 extensive experiments，并包含真实双臂机器人实验；但摘要未披露具体 benchmark 名称、任务配置与评价协议。
- **性能提升**：摘要报告 DBP 在性能上可匹配或超过 multi-step diffusion policies，同时推理速度最高可达 100x；并且在所述 challenging manipulation benchmarks 上持续优于现有 one-step baselines。摘要还说明 DBPO 能在在线设置中实现有效且稳定的策略改进，真实双臂机器人控制频率达到 105.2 Hz。
- **消融实验**：摘要未披露具体消融细节，也未说明各模块、训练目标或 stochastic interface 分别带来的独立贡献。

## 5. 局限与启发

- **当前局限**：从摘要可确认的方法优势主要集中在推理效率、在线可优化性与若干操作任务表现；但摘要未披露训练代价、样本效率、失败模式、安全性约束，以及在更广机器人形态或更复杂长时任务中的边界。
- **未来方向**：后续最需要补充的是具体 benchmark 与任务拆解、更多在线 RL 稳定性分析、系统化消融、理论证明，以及对训练开销和部署鲁棒性的更完整报告。
- **可迁移性**：摘要显示方法覆盖 offline imitation learning、online fine-tuning 和 real-world control，说明其有一定跨训练范式适用性；但跨机器人平台、不同传感输入、非 manipulation 任务和更广泛现实场景的可迁移性，摘要未给出直接证据。

## 6. 一句话总结

这项工作提出 DBP/DBPO 两阶段框架，用训练时内化 refinement 的原生一步式生成策略替代多步去噪策略，在保持多模态动作建模能力的同时显著提升在线机器人控制的推理速度与可优化性。

recognized_fields.abstract: Although multi-step generative policies achieve strong performance in robotic manipulation by modeling multimodal action distributions, they require multi-step iterative denoising at inference time. Each action therefore needs tens to hundreds of network function evaluations (NFEs), making them costly for high-frequency closed-loop control and online reinforcement learning (RL). To address this limitation, we propose a two-stage framework for native one-step generative policies that shifts refinement from inference to training. First, we introduce the Drift-Based Policy (DBP), which leverages fixed-point drifting objectives to internalize iterative refinement into the model parameters, yielding a one-step generative backbone by design while preserving multimodal action modeling capacity. Second, we develop Drift-Based Policy Optimization (DBPO), an online RL framework that equips the pretrained backbone with a compatible stochastic interface, enabling stable on-policy updates without sacrificing the one-step deployment property. Extensive experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed framework across offline imitation learning, online fine-tuning, and real-world control scenarios. DBP matches or exceeds the performance of multi-step diffusion policies while achieving up to $100\times$ faster inference. Experiments on a real-world dual-arm robot demonstrate reliable high-frequency control at 105.2 Hz.
